working: Open rates remain roughly double the 25-35% health benchmark: account average 52.02% across 100 campaigns, and the 2026-07-02 sends opened at 48.6% (Alumni eNews - July) and 50.1% (Staff-Client eNews - June, 8,894 sends).
Reminder sends keep outperforming originals: Alumni eNews Final Reminder (6/3) drove +65.2% lift (21.0 to 34.7 daily sessions) while the original 5/27 Alumni send was -9.8%.
Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/19, 9,731 sends) remains the best recent performer at +66.5% lift (17.0 to 28.3 daily sessions) with 53 direct sessions in 3 days.
GSC improved vs the 7/6 run: clicks 20 to 23, impressions 3,173 to 3,310, ranked pages 80 to 84 - yesterday's 7-day dip (3 clicks, position 23.45) looks like a short-window artifact.
All three data sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) fetched cleanly, and the window is now 1,100 days (31,990 sessions), giving the dashboard 89 newsletter sends of history instead of 8-10.
not_working: UTM leakage is total at full history scale: across all 89 sends in the window, email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d totals 5,528. GA4 credits Email just 4 sessions (0.0%) in 3 years vs Direct 16,280 (50.9%) because newsletter links carry no UTM tags.

The 7/2 double-send (Alumni July + Staff-Client June, same day, holiday week) reads -25.3% lift (17.0 to 12.7 daily sessions) with click rates of 0.78% and 0.76%, well under the 2.03% account average.
Recent click rates are sliding: the last four sends all clicked under 1.2% (0.69-1.18%) vs the account average of 2.03% and the historical best of 10.17% (Client eNews December 2023).
Organic Social is 5.4% of sessions across the full 1,100-day window - a marginal channel even at long range.
GSC impression-weighted position slipped slightly vs 7/6 (17.56 to 17.73) even as clicks and impressions grew.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email to all newsletter links - 5,528 direct sessions within 3 days of sends are being mis-credited to Direct, hiding email's real contribution (shown as 4 sessions in 3 years). Still the single highest-leverage fix.
Keep the reminder cadence and mid-week timing: the two +65% lifts landed Tue 5/19 and Wed 6/3; schedule a reminder about a week after each Alumni send and avoid holiday-adjacent dates like 7/2.
Stop same-day double-sends to different lists: the 7/2 Alumni + Staff-Client pairing split attention and landed at -25.3%; stagger them by several days.
Repromote event-style content - the all-time top lifts are event/alumni themed: Client eNews - August 2024 +1,263%, 2024 Alumni Events July +249%, September Alumni Event +199%.
Watch GSC position (17.56 to 17.73) while clicks grow; keep the retune pipeline moving on tracked pages losing average position.
summary: First long-range (1,100-day) pulse: 31,990 sessions, Direct 50.9% and Organic Search 33.6%. Opens stay strong (~52% average) but the 7/2 holiday double-send went -25.3% with sub-0.8% clicks, and total UTM leakage (0 email vs 5,528 direct post-send sessions across 89 sends) remains the biggest fix.
